summary.intent: Fix a crash caused by the browserless/chrome container failing to write to a bind-mounted host profile directory due to uid mismatch (discussion #374).
summary.outcome: Implemented a chmod 0o777 fix on the container profile directory after creation, added a helper function with safety documentation, a test, and shipped it via commit/push/PR.

repo: The browserless/chrome image runs Chrome as uid 999 (the 'chrome' user), which conflicts with host-owned bind-mounted directories.
Browser container lifecycle is managed in crates/browser/src/pool.rs and crates/browser/src/container.rs.
The workspace uses a shared Cargo workspace with tempfile available as a workspace-level dev dependency.
The non-container (direct browser) path also calls create_dir_all for sandbox profiles but does NOT need the chmod fix since it runs as the same user.

code.finding: Host profile directory for container is created here with default permissions (owned by current user), which caused the uid 999 container process to be unable to write to it.
Bind mount is added with :rw but no --user flag is passed, meaning container runs as uid 999 regardless of host directory ownership.
Second create_dir_all for sandbox profile dir belongs to the non-container path and does not need the world-writable chmod fix.

workflow: Passing --user to match host uid was considered but rejected because it conflicts with the browserless image's expected uid 999 and Chrome sandbox setup.
chmod 0o777 is the appropriate cross-platform fix (Docker, Podman, Apple Container) for container bind-mount permission issues when the container image has a fixed non-root uid.
The fix mirrors the manual setfacl workaround users had found independently, confirming the root cause diagnosis.

friction: Initial plan mode entry required a ToolSearch step, suggesting the EnterPlanMode tool was not immediately available.
Multiple reads of pool.rs were needed to locate the correct insertion points for the edit and the helper function, indicating the file is large and context was not fully retained between reads.
open_items: The per-session sandbox subdirectory creation (pool.rs:676-681) was identified as a candidate for the same chmod treatment but was not confirmed to be in the container code path — may need revisiting.
No integration test verifies that Chrome inside the container can actually write to the profile directory end-to-end; only the permission bits are unit-tested.
